item.title: When Disclosure Becomes a Security Scorecard: Responding to the 2027 Information Security Disclosure Expansion | Enki White Hat
item.description:
Recent hacking incidents are not limited to IT companies; they are occurring across all industries, including manufacturing, distribution, finance, public, education, and healthcare. The problem is that when an incident occurs, it doesn't end there. Trust erosion (customer attrition), transaction suspensions, and stock price/reputation shocks follow in a chain.
The government has drastically revised the system so that companies are not just saying 'We are good at security,' but are required to officially disclose security activities such as investments, personnel, checks, and certifications.
On January 9, the Ministry of Science and Technology announced [Partial Amendment to the Enforcement Decree of the Act on the Promotion of Information Security Industry], stating that it will expand the range of business operators subject to information security disclosure obligations and change the standards for calculating the number of users (Article 8, Paragraph 1 of the draft).
We have summarized the changes based on the 'Major Amendments to the Enforcement Decree of the Information Security Industry Act'.

item.title: EtherRAT Targeting Windows Disguised as a Game Mod Installer | Enki White Hat
item.description: EtherRAT Targeting Windows Disguised as a Game Mod Installer

item.title: How to Prepare | Enki White Hat
item.description: The three security transitions starting in 2026 are no longer a matter of choice. The mandatory information protection disclosure, the strengthening of the effectiveness of the Information Protection and Personal Information Protection Management System (ISMS脗路ISMS-P), and the changes in the cybersecurity evaluation indicators for government and public institutions each target different subjects but all point in the same direction. "It芒聙聶s about substance, not form; evidence, not words; evolving responses, not fixed standards." So what should organizations specifically prepare for?

item.description: Kimsuky Distributing Malicious Mobile App via QR Code

item.title: What is the biggest fear for security practitioners now? Common Denominators and Solutions for Incidents | Enki White Hat
item.description: Let's explore the 'real issues' revealed by 454 security practitioners and the 'effective response strategies' suggested by white hat hackers.

item.title: About the Hacking Defense Competition by CTF-Operating Company | Enki White Hat
item.description: A white-hat hacking company, which has been independently running hacking defense competitions, shares its accumulated know-how and insights. We introduce the value and operational points of CTFs, which go beyond simple events to contribute to enhancing security capabilities and cultivating talent.

item.title: Complete Analysis of the React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) Vulnerability | Enki White Hat
item.description: The vulnerability known as CVE-2025-55182, called React2Shell, occurs in the React Server Components (RSC) used in the latest versions of React. Attackers can perform Remote Code Execution (RCE) without any authentication (Pre-authentication) on web services using vulnerable versions, and the attack difficulty is not high. For these reasons, this vulnerability has received the highest severity score of 10.0 in the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS).

item.title: Kimsuky芒聙聶s Ongoing Evolution of KimJongRAT and Expanding Threats | Enki White Hat
item.description: Kimsuky芒聙聶s Ongoing Evolution of KimJongRAT and Expanding Threats
